events.text: [He's sitting on the floor in the middle of the labyrinth like it makes complete sense. He waves.]
You made it! Excellent. Most people get turned around at the third corridor.
I hear you want to end all existence to stop suffering.

events.label: choice_yes
events.text: Magnificent. Genuinely. I've heard a lot of responses to the absurd and that is easily the most dramatic.
I respect the commitment. Sisyphus would find it excessive. I find it endearing.

choices.text: This isn't endearing. This is grief.

events.text: I laugh because the universe is silent and it's either laugh or scream. I chose laugh. You're choosing something else. Both are valid.

events.label: yes_not_serious
events.text: I'm taking it very seriously. I'm just not pretending that seriousness is the same as meaning.

events.label: choice_complicated
events.text: It always is! That's the best part!
The universe gives you exactly zero framework and you have to build the whole thing out of wet cardboard and hope. It's hilarious.

choices.text: What if I'm out of hope?

events.label: complicated_hopeless
events.text: Revolt doesn't need hope. It just needs you to keep moving anyway. You're here. That's revolt.

events.label: complicated_hell
events.text: Kind of! And heaven. And a Tuesday. They're all the same material.
The trick is to keep playing even when you know the game has no winners.

events.label: choice_why_laugh
events.text: Because it's the most human possible response to an inhuman situation.
You're in so much pain that you want to erase all of existence. That's not laughable. That's enormous. I laugh so I don't forget how enormous it is.
